name: Mbombo
description: Mbombo, also known as Bumba, is the creator god in the mythology of the Bakongo people of the Democratic Republic of Congo. In the beginning, Mbombo existed alone in a primordial darkness and was overcome with a stomach ache. From this pain, he vomited forth the sun, moon, stars, and planets, bringing light and order to the universe. He then vomited forth living creatures, including humans, animals, and plants, along with other aspects of the world like mountains, rivers, and lightning. While Mbombo created all things, he didn't directly shape them. Instead, his children and other beings he created shaped and influenced the world as it is known today. He is often depicted as a white giant, representing his role as the origin of all things. Mbombo represents creation, the origin of life, and the power of transformation. He is a benevolent, albeit somewhat passive, creator figure in the Bakongo cosmology, initiating the world's existence but relying on his creations to further develop it. His story emphasizes the interconnectedness of all things and the importance of lineage and creation.
subtitle: The Vomiting Creator God

keyMyths.title: The Vomiting of Creation
keyMyths.description: Mbombo, suffering from a stomach ache in the primordial darkness, vomited forth the sun, moon, stars, and then all living things. This act marked the beginning of the world as we know it.

keyMyths.title: The Role of Mbombo's Children
keyMyths.description: While Mbombo created the initial elements of the world, it was his children and other beings he created who shaped and developed it further. They are responsible for the specific forms and features of the world.
keyMyths.source: Jan Vansina, 'Being Colonized: The Kuba Experience in Rural Congo, 1880-1960'

keyMyths.title: Mbombo's White Giant Form
keyMyths.description: Mbombo is often described as a white giant, a depiction symbolizing his purity, his role as the origin, and his immense power in bringing the universe into existence from nothingness.
keyMyths.source: John Middleton, 'Myth and Cosmos: Readings in Mythology and Symbolism'

sources.author: Jan Vansina
sources.work: Being Colonized: The Kuba Experience in Rural Congo, 1880-1960
